1. # ASP.NET Core 10 Breaking Changes
2. 
3. These changes affect projects using ASP.NET Core (Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web).
4. 
5. ## Source-Incompatible Changes
6. 
7. ### WebHostBuilder, IWebHost, and WebHost are obsolete
8. 
9. The legacy `WebHostBuilder` and related APIs are now marked obsolete. Migrate to the modern hosting model:
10. 
11. ```csharp
12. // Before (.NET 9)
13. var host = new WebHostBuilder()
14.     .UseKestrel()
15.     .UseStartup<Startup>()
16.     .Build();
17. 
18. // After (.NET 10)
19. var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);
20. // Configure services in builder.Services
21. var app = builder.Build();
22. // Configure middleware pipeline
23. app.Run();
24. ```
25. 
26. If still using `Startup` classes, the `WebApplication` model supports them via `builder.Host.ConfigureWebHostDefaults(...)` or inline configuration.
27. 
28. ### IActionContextAccessor and ActionContextAccessor are obsolete
29. 
30. These types are obsolete. Access `ActionContext` through dependency injection or the `HttpContext` instead:
31. ```csharp
32. // Before
33. services.AddSingleton<IActionContextAccessor, ActionContextAccessor>();
34. 
35. // After — use IHttpContextAccessor or inject ActionContext directly in filters/middleware
36. ```
37. 
38. ### Deprecation of WithOpenApi extension method
39. 
40. The `WithOpenApi()` extension method is deprecated. Use the built-in OpenAPI document generation in ASP.NET Core 10 instead.
41. 
42. ### IncludeOpenAPIAnalyzers property and MVC API analyzers deprecated
43. 
44. The `<IncludeOpenAPIAnalyzers>` MSBuild property is deprecated. Remove it from `.csproj` files. The analyzers are no longer needed with the new OpenAPI infrastructure.
45. 
46. ### IPNetwork and ForwardedHeadersOptions.KnownNetworks are obsolete
47. 
48. `IPNetwork` is obsolete. Use `System.Net.IPNetwork` (the new runtime type) and the new `KnownIpNetworks` property instead:
49. ```csharp
50. // Before
51. app.UseForwardedHeaders(new ForwardedHeadersOptions
52. {
53.     KnownNetworks = { new IPNetwork(IPAddress.Parse("10.0.0.0"), 8) }
54. });
55. 
56. // After — use KnownIpNetworks with the new System.Net.IPNetwork type
57. app.UseForwardedHeaders(new ForwardedHeadersOptions
58. {
59.     KnownIpNetworks = { new System.Net.IPNetwork(IPAddress.Parse("10.0.0.0"), 8) }
60. });
61. ```
62. 
63. ### Razor runtime compilation is obsolete
64. 
65. `AddRazorRuntimeCompilation()` is obsolete. Razor views and pages should be precompiled. For development, use hot reload (`dotnet watch`) instead.
66. 
67. ### Microsoft.Extensions.ApiDescription.Client package deprecated
68. 
69. The `Microsoft.Extensions.ApiDescription.Client` package is deprecated. Use the built-in OpenAPI client generation tooling instead.
70. 
71. ### Microsoft.OpenApi 2.x breaking API changes
72. 
73. `Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi 10.0` depends on `Microsoft.OpenApi` v2.x (from [`microsoft/OpenAPI.NET`](https://github.com/microsoft/OpenAPI.NET) repo), which has significant breaking API changes from v1.x. Projects that customize OpenAPI document generation using transformers or directly manipulate OpenAPI models will need code changes. Note: no official v1→v2 migration guide exists; the repo has a [v3 upgrade guide](https://github.com/microsoft/OpenAPI.NET/blob/main/docs/upgrade-guide-3.md) only. These changes are also not listed on the ASP.NET Core 10 breaking changes page.
74. 
75. **Namespace changes — `Microsoft.OpenApi.Models` and `Microsoft.OpenApi.Any` are completely removed:**
76. 
77. All types previously in these namespaces (`OpenApiSchema`, `OpenApiParameter`, `OpenApiResponse`, `OpenApiSecurityScheme`, etc.) have moved to the root `Microsoft.OpenApi` namespace. Replace all `using Microsoft.OpenApi.Models;` and `using Microsoft.OpenApi.Any;` with `using Microsoft.OpenApi;`.
78. 
79. ```csharp
80. // Before (.NET 9 — Microsoft.OpenApi v1.x)
81. using Microsoft.OpenApi.Any;
82. using Microsoft.OpenApi.Models;
83. 
84. // After (.NET 10 — Microsoft.OpenApi v2.x)
85. using Microsoft.OpenApi;          // ALL model types are here now
86. using System.Text.Json.Nodes;     // replaces OpenApiAny types
87. ```
88. 
89. **Key API changes:**
90. 
91. 1. **`OpenApiString` / `OpenApiAny` types removed** — Use `System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonNode` instead:
92.    ```csharp
93.    // Before
94.    parameter.Schema.Example = new OpenApiString("1.0");
95.    // After
96.    parameter.Schema.Example = JsonNode.Parse("\"1.0\"");
97.    ```
98. 
99. 2. **`OpenApiSecurityScheme.Reference` replaced** — Use `OpenApiSecuritySchemeReference` directly:
100.    ```csharp
101.    // Before
102.    var scheme = new OpenApiSecurityScheme
103.    {
104.        Reference = new OpenApiReference { Type = ReferenceType.SecurityScheme, Id = "oauth2" }
105.    };
106.    // After
107.    var scheme = new OpenApiSecuritySchemeReference("oauth2", null);
108.    ```
109. 
110. 3. **Collections now nullable** — `operation.Parameters`, `operation.Responses`, `document.Components.SecuritySchemes`, and other collection properties may be null and must be checked or initialized:
111.    ```csharp

131. 
132. ### Cookie login redirects disabled for known API endpoints
133.

136. This is generally the desired behavior for APIs, but may affect apps that relied on the redirect for API calls. To influence this behavior, you can manually add or check for `IApiEndpointMetadata` on specific endpoints.
137.

If handled exceptions include security-relevant events (authentication failures, authorization violations, injection attempts), suppressing diagnostics could create blind spots in security monitoring and audit logging. 1. # ASP.NET Core 10 Breaking Changes
2. 
3. These changes affect projects using ASP.NET Core (Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web).
4. 
5. ## Source-Incompatible Changes
6. 
7. ### WebHostBuilder, IWebHost, and WebHost are obsolete
8. 
9. The legacy `WebHostBuilder` and related APIs are now marked obsolete. Migrate to the modern hosting model:
10. 
11. ```csharp
12. // Before (.NET 9)
13. var host = new WebHostBuilder()
14.     .UseKestrel()
15.     .UseStartup<Startup>()
16.     .Build();
17. 
18. // After (.NET 10)
19. var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);
20. // Configure services in builder.Services
21. var app = builder.Build();
22. // Configure middleware pipeline
23. app.Run();
24. ```
25. 
26. If still using `Startup` classes, the `WebApplication` model supports them via `builder.Host.ConfigureWebHostDefaults(...)` or inline configuration.
27. 
28. ### IActionContextAccessor and ActionContextAccessor are obsolete
29. 
30. These types are obsolete. Access `ActionContext` through dependency injection or the `HttpContext` instead:
31. ```csharp
32. // Before
33. services.AddSingleton<IActionContextAccessor, ActionContextAccessor>();
34. 
35. // After — use IHttpContextAccessor or inject ActionContext directly in filters/middleware
36. ```
37. 
38. ### Deprecation of WithOpenApi extension method
39. 
40. The `WithOpenApi()` extension method is deprecated. Use the built-in OpenAPI document generation in ASP.NET Core 10 instead.
41. 
42. ### IncludeOpenAPIAnalyzers property and MVC API analyzers deprecated
43. 
44. The `<IncludeOpenAPIAnalyzers>` MSBuild property is deprecated. Remove it from `.csproj` files. The analyzers are no longer needed with the new OpenAPI infrastructure.
45. 
46. ### IPNetwork and ForwardedHeadersOptions.KnownNetworks are obsolete
47. 
48. `IPNetwork` is obsolete. Use `System.Net.IPNetwork` (the new runtime type) and the new `KnownIpNetworks` property instead:
49. ```csharp
50. // Before
51. app.UseForwardedHeaders(new ForwardedHeadersOptions
52. {
53.     KnownNetworks = { new IPNetwork(IPAddress.Parse("10.0.0.0"), 8) }
54. });
55. 
56. // After — use KnownIpNetworks with the new System.Net.IPNetwork type
57. app.UseForwardedHeaders(new ForwardedHeadersOptions
58. {
59.     KnownIpNetworks = { new System.Net.IPNetwork(IPAddress.Parse("10.0.0.0"), 8) }
60. });
61. ```
62. 
63. ### Razor runtime compilation is obsolete
64. 
65. `AddRazorRuntimeCompilation()` is obsolete. Razor views and pages should be precompiled. For development, use hot reload (`dotnet watch`) instead.
66. 
67. ### Microsoft.Extensions.ApiDescription.Client package deprecated
68. 
69. The `Microsoft.Extensions.ApiDescription.Client` package is deprecated. Use the built-in OpenAPI client generation tooling instead.

These changes are also not listed on the ASP.NET Core 10 breaking changes page.
74. 
75. **Namespace changes — `Microsoft.OpenApi.Models` and `Microsoft.OpenApi.Any` are completely removed:**
76. 
77. All types previously in these namespaces (`OpenApiSchema`, `OpenApiParameter`, `OpenApiResponse`, `OpenApiSecurityScheme`, etc.) have moved to the root `Microsoft.OpenApi` namespace. Here are the four breaking changes and fixes:

---

### 1. GenericShift.cs — Consistent shift behavior in generic math

In .NET 9, generic shift operators on small types (like `byte`) promoted to `int` internally, so `ShiftLeft<byte>(0xFF, 8)` produced `0` (0xFF00 truncated). In .NET 10, shifts are **consistent**: the amount is masked to the type's bit width (`8 & 7 = 0`), so the result is now `0xFF`.

**Fix:** If you depend on the old promotion-then-truncation semantics, widen explicitly:

```csharp
static T ShiftLeft<T>(T value, int amount) where T : IShiftOperators<T, int, T>
    => value << amount;  // .NET 10: amount masked to type width

// If you need the old "promote to int" behavior, cast explicitly before shifting
```

---

### 2. ApiClient.cs — Streaming HTTP responses enabled by default in Blazor WASM

In .NET 10, **streaming HTTP responses are enabled by default in browser HTTP clients**. `ReadAsStreamAsync()` now returns a true streaming (unbuffered) stream. The **synchronous** `stream.Read(...)` call will throw `NotSupportedException` on a browser streaming response.

**Fix:** Use async reads:

```csharp
var response = await httpClient.GetAsync(url);
var stream = await response.Content.ReadAsStreamAsync();
var buffer = new byte[4096];
int bytesRead = await stream.ReadAsync(buffer, 0, buffer.Length);  // async
```

---

### 3. Archiver.cs — PaxTarEntry no longer includes atime/ctime by default

In .NET 10, `GnuTarEntry`/`PaxTarEntry` **no longer auto-populate `AccessTime` and `ChangeTime`**. Your assumption that they are "set to now" is no longer true.

**Fix:** Set them explicitly:

```csharp
var entry = new PaxTarEntry(TarEntryType.RegularFile, "data.txt");
var now = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;
entry.AccessTime = now;
entry.ChangeTime = now;
archive.WriteEntry(entry);
```

---

### 4. App.csproj + HTTP/3 — HTTP/3 stripped by trimmer

With `<PublishTrimmed>true</PublishTrimmed>`, **HTTP/3 support is completely disabled** in .NET 10 — the implementation code is removed by the trimmer. Your backend connections over HTTP/3 will silently fall back or fail.

**Fix:** Add `<Http3Support>` to your `.csproj`:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <PublishTrimmed>true</PublishTrimmed>
  <Http3Support>true</Http3Support>
</PropertyGroup>
```

---

### Summary

| Code | Breaking Change | Severity |
|------|----------------|----------|
| GenericShift | Shift amount now masked to type width | Behavioral — silent result change |
| ApiClient | Streaming responses default on in browser | Runtime `NotSupportedException` |
| Archiver | atime/ctime no longer auto-set | Behavioral — silent data loss |
| App.csproj | HTTP/3 code stripped when trimmed | Silent connectivity downgrade |
